article_title: NetHack 5.0 Ships 11 Years After 3.6, Replacing Yacc and Lex With Lua and Rebasing the Roguelike on C99
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: NetHack 5.0 Ships 11 Years After 3.6, Replacing Yacc and Lex With Lua and Rebasing the Roguelike on C99
content_preview.summary: The DevTeam released NetHack 5.0.0 on May 2, ending an 11-year wait since 3.6.0 and folding more than 3,100 fixes, a Lua-driven dungeon and quest pipeline, and full C99 source into the 39-year-old roguelike.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The NetHack DevTeam released NetHack 5.0.0 on May 2, 2026, the project's first major version since 3.6.0 in 2015. [The Register](https://www.theregister.com/2026/05/05/nethack_5/) headlined the milestone as the classic ASCII roguelike debuting version 5.0 "just 11 years after last major release." The release was published as `NetHack-5.0.0_Released` on the project's [GitHub repository](https://github.com/NetHack/NetHack/releases/tag/NetHack-5.0.0_Released) and tagged by maintainer @...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Solid News piece, 767 words. Strong reporting on architectural changes (C99, cross-compilation, yacc/lex → Lua), gameplay additions, and platform coverage. The 'Why It Matters' framing is editorial-analytical without being editorializing.

source_verification.source-0_theregister: Verified verbatim: '11 years after last major release' framing, May 2 release date, 'C99... became obsolete in 2011' wry note, 'genetic engineer which attacks by inflicting random mutations', royal jelly egg revival, wet towel poison cloud reduction, Archaeologist fedora luck bonus, Windows on Arm port (tested on Qualcomm and AMD), MSDOS and Amiga binaries.
source_verification.source-1_github: Verified: nhmall released NetHack-5.0.0_Released on May 2 17:17, source code C99 compliance, cross-compiling support, 'yacc and lex'-based level/dungeon compilers and makedefs replaced with Lua text alternatives, 'over 3100 fixes and changes' in doc/fixes5-0-0.txt.
source_verification.source-2_slashdot: Verified: '39 years old', AmigaDOS 3.0 / 68000 support, optional tutorial in early phases, Lua for dungeon generation, MSDOS/Amiga binaries, multi-platform source build list (Linux, Windows 8-11, AmigaDOS, MacOS, Windows CE, OS/2, BeOS, VMS, Unixes).

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All quoted material traced verbatim. Numerical claims (3,100 fixes, 11 years, 39 years) all corroborated. Architectural details match the GitHub release notes verbatim.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and technically literate. The Register's wryness is reproduced via direct quote rather than editorial mimicry.
editor_notes.originality: No prior NetHack archive coverage. Fresh story.
concerns: games.slashdot.org is not on the project allowlist. Slashdot is a long-established tech news aggregator with editorial moderation; the cited Slashdot piece is a discussion thread that includes verified user MilenCent's first-hand observations about 5.0 specifics. Recommend adding to allowlist.
recommendations: Document the allowlist gap in a corrections file (advisory).
Add games.slashdot.org (or slashdot.org broadly) to config/source_allowlist.txt.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS. Cryptography, sources, and content all clean. Only finding is the Slashdot allowlist gap.
